edge: Visual recognition in low-data regimes requires deep neural networks to learn generalized representations from limited training samples.
Recently, CLIP-based methods have shown promising few-shot performance benefited from the contrastive language-image pre-training.
In this paper, we propose CaFo, a Cascade of Foundation models that incorporates diverse prior knowledge of various pre-training paradigms for better few-shot learning.

We leverage GPT-3 to produce textual inputs for prompting CLIP with rich downstream linguistic semantics. Then, we generate synthetic images via DALL-E to expand the few-shot training data without any manpower.
Our CaFo incorporates CLIP’s DINO’s vision-contrastive knowledge, DALL-E’s vision-generative knowledge, and GPT-3’s language-generative knowledge.
By such collaboration, CaFo can fully unleash the potential of different pre-training methods and unify them to perform state-of-the-art for few-shot classification.

edge: Meta-learning is proposed to improve the few-shot adaptation ability of the models.
CLIP exhibiting strong zero-shot adaptation performance.
CoOp is proposed as a prompt tuning adaptation method by optimizing a set of learnable prompt tokens.
CoCoOp proposes to inject textual branch with visual signals.
VT-CLIP proposes to train an intermediate network to generate image tokens as conditional inputs for the textual vectors.
CLIP-Adapter is introduced to fine-tune CLIP by applying lightweight residual-style adapters.
Tip-Adapter is then proposed as a training-free adaptation method with a constructed key-value cache model.
The initialization method with a constructed key-value cache model is a better way to initialize CLIP-Adapter and allows for much faster convergence when fine-tuning.
CALIP proposes a parameter-free attention to enhance CLIP in a zero-shot manner, and its parametric solution achieves higher few-shot accuracy.
SuS-X constructs a dynamic support set and extends Tip-Adapter by leveraging image-text distances, enhancing CLIP.

Different from all existing methods, the integration of powerful pre-training paradigms with CLIP and collaboration with customized pipelines are discussed in the Cascade of Foundation Models section.

Contrastive Vision-Language Pre-training involves contrastive learning between vision and language to map both modalities into the same embedding space, aiming to minimize embedding distances of images and their textual descriptions, while maximizing those unpaired ones.
Driven by web-scale datasets, Pre-training Paradigms like CLIP focus on minimizing embedding distances of images and their textual descriptions, while maximizing those unpaired ones.
The cross-modal alignment discriminates images of different categorizes by the texts with different semantics.

GPT-3 is able to output prompts with rich linguistic semantics for vision-language models. CLIP utilizes handcrafted templates as prompts, e.g., 'a photo of a [CLASS]', which however lacks sufficient textual semantics to align with input images. We thus leverage GPT-3 to produce CLIP’s prompts to better align with visual information from images.

CLIP utilizes handcrafted templates as prompts, e.g., 'a photo of a [CLASS]', which however lacks sufficient textual semantics to align with input images. We thus leverage GPT-3 to produce CLIP’s prompts to better align with visual information from images.
Generative Vision-Language Pre-training. Learned from millions of image-caption pairs, the DALL-E series can generate language-conditioned images in a zero-shot manner.
They are pre-trained to autoregressively predict the encoded image tokens from the textual tokens of the captions.
With such language-generative knowledge, the pre-trained DALL-E can be viewed as a free lunch to enlarge the training data without any manpower.
Considering publicity, we select DALL-E-mini [14] as the representative among DALL-E models.

We adopt DALL-E to generate synthetic images to expand the limited few-shot training samples. Then, we construct the cache model with two kinds of keys to adaptively fuse the knowledge from CLIP and DINO.
To cascade different pre-training paradigms, we introduce CaFo with a pipeline of 'Prompt, Generate, Cache', which respectively unleashes the powers of different self-supervised knowledge.
